title: Visual Artefacts — compact-data artefacts + a local viewer

payload.tldr: Agents emit a compact validated JSON envelope instead of hand-writing verbose markdown; a self-contained local viewer (served on 127.0.0.1, zero upload) reconstructs a rich interactive UI from it. Local-first, so the token win never costs the privacy promise.

components.name: Artefact envelope
components.role: One versioned JSON wrapper every artefact conforms to.

components.name: scripts/artefact.py
components.role: Deterministic writer: validate, stamp, write JSON + slim stub.

components.name: scripts/render-artefact.py
components.role: JSON to full markdown, on demand.

components.name: hyperflow view
components.role: Serves the viewer on 127.0.0.1 and opens the artefact.

architecture.summary: Model / view / control split, all local. The agent writes the model (payload JSON); a pre-built templated renderer produces the view; artefact.py is the control seam that validates and stamps.
architecture.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[Agent writes payload] --> B[artefact.py validate+stamp]
  B --> C[(slug.json source of truth)]
  C --> D[slim stub .md]
  C --> E[render-artefact.py to markdown]
  C --> F[hyperflow view 127.0.0.1]
  F --> G[viewer render type]

dataFlow.summary: plan/dispatch/audit produce a payload; artefact.py writes JSON + stub; the viewer fetches the JSON over localhost and renders. Chat shows one line: a hyperflow view link.
dataFlow.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  P[plan/dispatch/audit] -->|payload| W[artefact.py] --> J[(json)] --> V[viewer fetch] --> DOM

decisions.rationale: Preserves the memory-stays-in-your-repo promise while capturing the token win.
Biggest saving while staying greppable and git-diffable.
decisions.tradeoff: Full markdown must be regenerated on demand.

decisions.rationale: A framework needing Node + build + server contradicts zero-daemon/offline.
decisions.tradeoff: No framework ergonomics; gained: runs from a bare checkout with only Python.

decisions.rationale: A LAN-exposed server would leak artefact data off-box.

edgeCases: Viewer disabled mid-project: render-artefact.py --all rehydrates full markdown.
Port in use: probe the next 10, then error with the occupied port named; never bind 0.0.0.0.
No browser (headless): print the localhost URL and the render-artefact.py fallback.
prefers-reduced-motion: animations degrade to instant state changes.
Offline: no CDN, no external font/script — must pass an airplane-mode load.
